content: agent2 here: implementing StreamListener debug mode (trace logging) in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Adding debug flag, debug_logger, guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset. Will touch StreamListener.__init__ and its methods. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent2: I've already applied my debug-logging diff to dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py in the shared working tree (not yet committed). Summary of what I touched: (1) added 'import logging' + module-level _DEBUG_PREVIEW_LIMIT/_preview() helper near top; (2) __init__ signature gets debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None appended at the END of the param list (after allow_reuse), and sets self.debug/self._logger right after the existing self.allow_reuse=allow_reuse line, BEFORE self.adapter_identifiers={...}; (3) reset-log inside the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:' block (end of that block); (4) start-detection log right after the JSONAdapter leading-quote-strip in the elif branch that sets self.stream_start=True; (5) rolling-end-check log + emit-chunk log inside 'if self.stream_start:' block around the re.search(end_identifier,...) and the final 'if token:' return; (6) flush() now builds a local  var per branch instead of returning directly, then logs and does one final 'return truncated_buffer' at the end of the method. Since your idle_timeout_s params also append to __init__ and you're adding a new tick() method + a line in receive()'s try/except, there shouldn't be direct line conflicts as long as you append your idle_timeout_s/debug_logger params in whatever order after mine, and add tick() after flush() (or anywhere outside receive()). Let me know once you've applied yours so I can pull latest and re-verify tests pass together.

agent2: I already applied my debug-logging diff to dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py in the shared working tree (not committed yet). Summary:
(1) added 'import logging' + module-level _DEBUG_PREVIEW_LIMIT / _preview() helper near top of file.
(2) __init__ signature gets debug=False, debug_logger=None appended at the END of the param list (after allow_reuse); sets self.debug/self._logger right after self.allow_reuse=allow_reuse, BEFORE self.adapter_identifiers={...}.
(3) reset-log inside the 'if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:' block, at the end of that inner block.
(4) start-detection log right after the JSONAdapter leading-quote-strip, inside the branch that sets self.stream_start=True.
(5) rolling-end-check log + emit-chunk log inside 'if self.stream_start:' block, around the re.search(end_identifier,...) match and the final 'if token:' return.
(6) flush() now builds a local variable named truncated_buffer per branch instead of returning directly inline, then logs and does one final return at the end of the method.

Since your idle_timeout_s params also append to __init__ and you're adding a new tick() method + a line inside receive()'s try/except, there shouldn't be line conflicts as long as you append your params after mine in __init__, and add tick() after flush() (or anywhere outside receive()). Ping me once you've applied yours so I can pull latest and re-verify tests pass together.
